name: Perceus — Compile-Time Reference Counting with Reuse
summary: Daan Leijen et al. (Microsoft Research): the compiler statically inserts inc/dec/reset/reuse operations for reference-counted memory in Koka. Reuse analysis enables in-place updates of pure-functional data without allocation. Frame-Limited Reuse, FIP, and TRMC make the system production-strong.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Perceus is the canonical compile-time reference counting system, used by Koka. The compiler statically inserts `inc`/`dec`/`reset`/`reuse` operations into the IR — ordinary operations that the optimiser can analyse, move, and elide. The pure-functional source language is compiled to imperative code with explicit lifetime management.

The core insight: with linear-resource analysis, the compiler can prove when a value's reference count is exactly 1 and reuse the allocation in place — turning a functional update into a destructive update without changing the source language's semantics. The result: Koka can match imperative-style performance from a purely functional source.

sources.label: Perceus: Garbage Free Reference Counting with Reuse (PLDI 2021)

sections.title: Frame-Limited Reuse, FIP, TRMC
sections.content:
Three significant developments since 2022:

**Frame-Limited Reuse** (Lorenzen-Leijen, ICFP 2022): drop-guided reuse formally proven *frame-limited* — peak heap grows by at most a constant factor — replacing fragile size-pairing reuse with a robust derivation rule.

**FIP / FP² calculus** (ICFP 2023): the `fip` keyword statically certifies a function uses zero allocation and constant stack; covers splay trees, finger trees, merge/quick sort, and generically derived in-place `map`.

**TRMC + first-class constructor contexts** (POPL 2023; JFP 2024): tail-recursion modulo context generalises TRMc to CPS, monoids, and evaluation contexts; the hybrid implementation uses Perceus heap semantics so call/cc and effect handlers stay sound.

Together these turn Perceus from a refcount-with-reuse system into a complete *compile-time imperative-from-functional* programming model — purely functional source, in-place mutation in the binary, with formal frame bounds.
